stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of Meta-Llama-3-8B on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key NLP/ML tags including 'transformers', 'llama', and 'text-generation'.
This indicates ongoing community interest and evaluation of a major open-source LLM model.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The content highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face.
It is categorized under core NLP/ML pipelines like 'text-generation' and utilizes 'transformers' and 'safetensors'.
This indicates active community interest and benchmarking around Meta's latest Llama iteration.

stories.title: NYT slams Microsoft for building copyright-infringing supercomputer for OpenAI
stories.author: Ashley Belanger
bullet_points: The New York Times is reportedly shifting its copyright claims against Microsoft and OpenAI.
This shift follows a recent Supreme Court ruling that was unfavorable to Sony in copyright matters.
The core issue involves allegations that Microsoft built a supercomputer for OpenAI that infringes on copyrights.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized for text generation and conversational AI tasks.
It utilizes standard ML components such as transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V4-Pro
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the DeepSeek-V4-Pro model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized under text-generation, indicating its primary function as a large language model.
It is tagged with relevant ML technologies like 'transformers' and 'safetensors'.

stories.title: Ollama's highest performance on Apple Silicon yet with MLX
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama has achieved its peak performance on Apple Silicon using the MLX engine.
The performance improvements result in higher quality model outputs.
The updated engine also provides faster response times and reduced memory usage.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, is integrating MLX for performance optimization.
This integration specifically targets Apple Silicon, offering a faster execution experience.
The feature is currently available in a preview build, indicating active development.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging apps.
It connects users to local AI coding agents.
A key feature is that all AI processing runs entirely on the user's local device for privacy.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command for running coding tools locally or in the cloud.
It supports various coding models, including Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or config files.

stories.title: South Korea plans to train entire military as "drone warriors"
stories.author: Jeremy Hsu
bullet_points: South Korea is planning a large-scale military training initiative focused on drones.
The military intends to train its entire force to utilize drones as a 'universal combat tool'.
The scope of the plan involves half a million personnel undergoing this specialized training.

stories.title: When Does Combining Language Models Help? A Co-Failure Ceiling on Routing, Voting, and Mixture-of-Agents Across 67 Frontier Models
stories.author: Josef Chen
bullet_points: The paper establishes a theoretical 'Co-Failure Ceiling' limiting the performance gains of multi-model LLM systems (like routing or voting).
The maximum accuracy of these ensemble methods is bounded by $1 - \beta$, where $\beta$ is the rate at which all constituent models fail on the same query.
Standard diagnostic metrics, such as pairwise error correlation ($\rho$), are insufficient to identify this critical failure rate ($\beta$).

stories.title: Prompt Injection in Automated Résumé Screening with Large Language Models: Single and Multi-Injection Settings
stories.author: Stefanus Jasin
bullet_points: The research investigates prompt injection vulnerabilities in LLMs used for automated résumé screening.
Prompt injection is defined as subtle, self-promotional text designed to manipulate LLM evaluations without adding new qualifications.
Experiments demonstrate that prompt injection reliably improves applicant rankings, particularly when résumé quality is similar across candidates.

stories.title: Reinforcement Learning without Ground-Truth Solutions can Improve LLMs
stories.author: Yuxiong He
bullet_points: Introduces RiVER, a new framework for training Large Language Models (LLMs) using Reinforcement Learning without requiring ground-truth solutions.
Addresses a major limitation of traditional RLVR methods, which depend on known correct answers for reward assignment.
Uses deterministic execution feedback as continuous-valued supervision for score-based optimization tasks.

stories.title: Hallucination in World Models is Predictable and Preventable
stories.author: Xiaolong Wang
bullet_points: The paper addresses the critical issue of 'hallucination' in generative world models, where simulated futures drift from real-world dynamics.
The authors hypothesize that hallucinations occur in poorly covered regions of the state-action space, suggesting a data-centric solution.
They introduce MMBench2, a large-scale dataset designed to test and guide the mitigation of these model hallucinations.

stories.title: Previewing GPT‑5.6 Sol: a next-generation model
stories.author: minimaxir
bullet_points: The news concerns a preview of GPT-5.6 Sol, indicating a potential next-generation model from OpenAI.
The announcement is sourced from Hacker News, suggesting it is relevant to the developer and AI community.
The provided link points to an OpenAI deployment safety page, confirming the official nature of the preview.

stories.title: Improved performance and model support with GGUF
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama 0.30 release brings improved performance and enhanced GGUF model compatibility.
The update leverages llama.cpp to broaden model support across various hardware configurations.
It augments Ollama's MLX engine specifically for better performance on Apple silicon.

stories.title: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: NVIDIA has introduced Nemotron 3 Ultra.
The model is specifically engineered for high-throughput reasoning tasks.
It is designed to support complex, long-running agent workflows.

stories.title: OpenJarvis: a local-first personal AI is now available to run with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenJarvis v1.0 is released, providing an open-source framework for personal AI agents.
The key feature is that these agents can run locally on the user's own hardware.
It features built-in support for Ollama, facilitating local LLM execution.

stories.title: Antibiotic "megacluster" discovery provides new strategy to fight superbugs
stories.author: Beth Mole
bullet_points: Discovery of an antibiotic 'megacluster' offers a new approach to combating antibiotic-resistant bacteria (superbugs).
The finding is considered an exciting development in the ongoing effort to replenish the supply of effective antibiotics.
This research points toward novel strategies in the critical area of antimicrobial resistance (AMR).

stories.title: Error-Conditioned Neural Solvers
stories.author: Jeong Joon Park
bullet_points: Neural surrogate models for solving PDEs are fast but lack inherent mechanisms to correct constraint violations or extrapolate reliably.
Existing hybrid methods improve physical correctness by targeting the PDE residual but suffer from the computational overhead of classical optimizers.
The research proposes a novel approach (implied by 'We show, theore') to address the limitations of purely statistical neural solvers while maintaining physical accuracy.

stories.title: Language-Based Digital Twins for Elderly Cognitive Assistance
stories.author: Hiroko H. Dodge
bullet_points: Proposes a language-based digital twin framework for personalized cognitive health monitoring.
Leverages Large Language Models (LLMs) to mimic the conversational behavior of elderly individuals.
Aims to use conversational patterns as non-invasive biomarkers for early detection of Mild Cognitive Impairment (MCI).

stories.title: Empowering GUI Agents via Autonomous Experience Exploration and Hindsight Experience Utilization for Task Planning
stories.author: Jun Zhao
bullet_points: The research addresses limitations in small, cost-efficient Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) used for GUI agent task planning.
It introduces the PEEU method, which uses autonomous experience exploration and Hindsight Experience Utilization to improve planning.
The goal is to enable small open-source agents to effectively handle complex, repetitive GUI tasks across different websites.

stories.title: AI Healthcare Chatbots as Information Infrastructure: A Large-Scale Study of User-Reported Breakdowns
stories.author: Masooda Bashir
bullet_points: The study analyzes over 15,000 user reviews across 59 AI healthcare chatbots to assess their real-world performance.
Three primary recurring breakdowns were identified: access barriers/service unreliability, and user experience/interaction quality.
The research highlights the need for further study on the impact and functioning of AI chatbots in health information seeking.

stories.title: DanceOPD: On-Policy Generative Field Distillation
stories.author: Tat-Seng Chua
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of unifying diverse capabilities (T2I, local/global editing) in modern image generation models.
Identifies a core problem: editing capabilities often conflict with or degrade base text-to-image performance.
Introduces 'DanceOPD' as a new method designed to effectively compose these conflicting image generation capabilities.

stories.title: When are likely answers right? On Sequence Probability and Correctness in LLMs
stories.author: Jonas Geiping
bullet_points: The paper investigates the fundamental relationship between sequence probability (as predicted by LLMs) and factual correctness.
It analyzes how different LLM decoding methods shift probability mass toward potential outputs.
The research aims to quantify when the high probability assigned by a model actually corresponds to a correct answer.

stories.title: Error-Conditioned Neural Solvers
stories.author: Jeong Joon Park
bullet_points: Neural surrogate models for solving PDEs struggle with constraint violations and extrapolation.
Current hybrid methods improve physical correctness but suffer from the computational cost of classical optimizers.
The research proposes a novel approach to address the limitations of purely statistical neural solvers in physical modeling.

stories.title: Empowering GUI Agents via Autonomous Experience Exploration and Hindsight Experience Utilization for Task Planning
stories.author: Jun Zhao
bullet_points: Addresses limitations in small, cost-efficient Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) used for GUI agent tasks.
Introduces PEEU (Planning Experience Exploration and Utilization) to improve task planning and cross-website generalization.
Focuses on enabling autonomous GUI agents to handle complex, repetitive tasks, balancing efficiency with planning capability.

stories.title: Empowering GUI Agents via Autonomous Experience Exploration and Hindsight Experience Utilization for Task Planning
stories.author: Jun Zhao
bullet_points: The research addresses limitations in small, open-source Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) used for GUI agent task planning.
It introduces the PEEU method to enhance planning by autonomously exploring environments and utilizing hindsight experience.
The work focuses on enabling cost-efficient and privacy-preserving AI agents to handle complex, repetitive GUI tasks.

stories.title: LLM-Based Examination of Eligibility Criteria from Securities Prospectuses at the German Central Bank
stories.author: Christian Hänig
bullet_points: The research addresses the resource-intensive task of verifying security eligibility criteria using German Central Bank prospectuses.
It proposes using Large Language Models (LLMs) to overcome limitations of traditional NER methods (e.g., OCR noise, linguistic variance).
The application focuses on automating information extraction from lengthy, semi-structured, and potentially bilingual financial documents.

stories.title: Beyond Surface Forms: A Comprehensive, Mechanism-Oriented Taxonomy of Indirect Linguistic Encoding for LLM-Based Coded Language Detection
stories.author: Paul Jen-Hwa Hu
bullet_points: Addresses the growing problem of indirect linguistic encoding (ILE) used by users to evade moderation and surveillance on social media.
Proposes a new, mechanism-oriented taxonomy to categorize these obfuscation techniques (like algospeak and euphemisms) based on underlying operations, rather than communicative goals.
Relevant to the development and robustness of LLMs in content moderation and adversarial language detection.

stories.title: How Surprising Is Historical Italian to Language Models? Tokenization Tax, Comprehension Tax, and a Simple Mitigation
stories.author: Maria Levchenko
bullet_points: The paper addresses the poorly understood challenge of LLMs processing historical languages, specifically using Italian as a case study.
It introduces a diagnostic framework to decompose historical language difficulty into four dimensions, including tokenization cost and predictive uncertainty.
The research aims to provide a method for mitigating the performance degradation LLMs experience with non-modern or historical texts.

stories.title: The Geometry of Updates: Fisher Alignment at Vocabulary Scale
stories.author: John Sweeney
bullet_points: Addresses a specific challenge in LLM fine-tuning: training-free source selection for models sharing a vocabulary.
Focuses on scientific string domains (SMILES, protein, genomic sequences) where traditional metrics fail due to 'activation-dark' regimes.
Proposes a novel method ('Fisher Alignment') to overcome the computational limitations of update-geometry metrics at vocabulary scale.

stories.title: LMs as Task-Specific Knowledge Bases: An Interpretability Analysis
stories.author: Mor Geva
bullet_points: The paper investigates the hypothesis that Language Models (LMs) function as task-specific knowledge bases.
A key property of traditional knowledge bases (consistent retrieval for the same fact) is tested against LMs.
The findings suggest that LMs encode factual knowledge in a manner that is specific to the task they are performing.

stories.title: U.S. government will decide who gets to use GPT-5.6
stories.author: alain94040
bullet_points: The U.S. government is set to determine access to the GPT-5.6 model.
This indicates increasing governmental oversight and control over advanced AI technology.
The news suggests a significant shift in how cutting-edge AI capabilities will be distributed.

stories.title: Instrument AI Agent Decision Tracing with OpenTelemetry
stories.author: toxsec
bullet_points: The article discusses instrumenting AI agent decision tracing using OpenTelemetry.
This addresses critical operational concerns, such as debugging unintended actions by AI agents.
It focuses on observability practices within the growing field of autonomous AI systems.

stories.title: Functional doesn't mean correct. That's the biggest risk with AI-generated code.
stories.author: cyclopt_dimitrisk
bullet_points: AI-generated code can execute successfully ('functional') without being logically correct.
The primary risk highlighted is the presence of hidden failure modes in AI-written code.
The article addresses the gap between code execution and functional correctness in AI-assisted development.
